description_long: This structure is a traditional trullo, characterized by its thick, dry-stone walls and a domed, conical roof made from irregularly shaped stones. The exterior is composed of roughly hewn, pale stones stacked without visible mortar, giving it a rustic and historic appearance. The front features a small, weathered wooden door with vertical planks, reinforced by horizontal beams and a metal latch, set within a slightly arched stone doorway. A plaque or sign is mounted on the door, and the base is surrounded by patches of grass and earth, suggesting it is set in a natural or semi-urban environment. The roof is entirely stone, with no visible pinnacle or decorative finial at the apex. The interior, visible from the cutaway views, shows the same stone construction, emphasizing the robust, sheltering nature of the building.
description: A rustic trullo with thick, pale stone walls and a conical stone roof, featuring a small wooden door set in an arched entryway.
description_view_0: The front view shows the arched wooden door, stone walls, and a plaque on the door, with some grass and earth at the base.
description_view_1: This side cutaway reveals the interior stonework and the thickness of the walls, with the conical roof structure visible.
description_view_2: A partial rear-side view, showing the inside of the trullo and the back of the wooden door, highlighting the stone dome's construction.
description_view_3: A rear three-quarter view, focusing on the stone wall curvature and the side of the entryway, with less detail on the door.
